title: SentinAI — Node Health Detector
description: Fleet health, per-component Day 0/Day N panels with DCGM, node_exporter, kubelet, kube-state-metrics. Workload critical path tables.

panels.title: Fleet Health Score
panels.description: Fraction of healthy nodes. 1.0 = all healthy.

panels.title: Node x Component Status
panels.description: Each row = node/component. Scan for red.

panels.title: Node x Check Status (Selected)
panels.description: Per-check drill-down for selected nodes.

panels.title: GPU Operator: GPU Count per Node
panels.description: count(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP) per instance = GPUs visible after operator injection. Expected: 8 for DGX B200. 0 = driver failure.

panels.title: GPU Operator: Driver & CUDA Version
panels.description: DCGM exporter exposes driver version as a label. All nodes should report the same version to avoid NCCL conflicts.

panels.title: Network Operator: IB Port State
panels.description: node_infiniband_state from node_exporter. 5 = LinkUp (Active). All HCA ports must be 5 (Active) for RDMA traffic.

panels.title: Network Operator: IB Physical State
panels.description: node_infiniband_physical_state. 5 = LinkUp. Physical != Active means cable/switch issue.

panels.title: SR-IOV: Virtual Function Count
panels.description: sriov_vf_count (from sriov-network-operator metrics or SentinAI). 0 VFs = SR-IOV not configured or VFs stuck in INIT.

panels.title: SR-IOV: Network Interfaces (VFs)
panels.description: node_network_up for VF interfaces. 1 = link detected.

panels.title: Kubelet: Running Pods & Containers
panels.description: kubelet_running_pods and kubelet_running_containers. If both are 0 on a healthy node, kubelet may not be scheduling.

panels.title: Kubelet: Pod Start Duration (p99)
panels.description: kubelet_pod_start_duration_seconds — time from API admission to running. Spikes indicate scheduling or image pull bottlenecks.

panels.title: Container Runtime: Operations/sec
panels.description: kubelet_runtime_operations_total rate — create, start, stop, remove. Flat line on a busy node = runtime frozen.

panels.title: Container Runtime: Error Rate
panels.description: kubelet_runtime_operations_errors_total rate. Spikes = runtime issues (image pull failures, OCI errors, device plugin failures).

panels.title: System: Kernel & Boot Time
panels.description: node_boot_time_seconds — when node last booted. Recent boot after unexpected reboot = investigate MCE/panic.

panels.title: System: HugePages Configuration
panels.description: node_memory_HugePages_Total / Free. HugePages = 0 means BIOS/kernel not configured for RDMA pinned memory.

panels.title: GPU Compute: Utilization
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL — GPU engine utilization (0-100%). Under training load, expect 90-100%. Low util during job = straggler.

panels.title: GPU Compute: SM Clock vs Max
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_SM_CLOCK vs MAX. >10% delta = thermal/power throttle (Silent Killer).

panels.title: GPU Compute: Temperature
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP. B200 liquid: max 70C. B200 air: max 83C. >90C = cordon.

panels.title: GPU Compute: Power Draw
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE. B200 TDP = 1000W. Sustained near TDP = normal under load.

panels.title: GPU Memory: Framebuffer Usage
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED / FREE. B200 = 192 GB HBM3e per GPU.

panels.title: GPU Memory: ECC Errors
panels.description: DBE (double-bit) = P0 CRITICAL, immediate cordon. SBE = monitor trend.

panels.title: GPU Memory: Retired Pages
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_RETIRED_SBE/DBE. SBE < 60 = OK. DBE must be 0. Rising SBE = HBM degradation trend.

panels.title: GPU Memory: Row Remap
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_ROW_REMAP_FAILURE. Must be 0. >0 = HBM bank unrepairable.

panels.title: XID Error Timeline
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_XID_ERRORS — every GPU error on a single time axis. Critical: 48 (ECC DBE), 79 (off bus), 74 (NVLink), 61-64, 94-95.

panels.title: NVLink: CRC Flit Errors
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_NVLINK_CRC_FLIT_ERROR_COUNT_TOTAL. Low-level link noise. Sustained increase = cable/connector degradation.

panels.title: NVLink: Replay Errors
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_NVLINK_REPLAY_ERROR_COUNT_TOTAL. Re-transmissions. Small counts = noise. Sustained = NVLink degradation.

panels.title: NVLink: Recovery Errors
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_NVLINK_RECOVERY_ERROR_COUNT_TOTAL. >0 = link went down and recovered. P0 signal — link unreliable, potential NVLink fabric failure (XID 74).

panels.title: PCIe: Link Generation
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_PCIE_LINK_GEN. Expected: 5 (Gen5). Gen4 = 50% BW loss. Gen3 = 75% loss. Silent performance killer.

panels.title: PCIe: Link Width
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_PCIE_LINK_WIDTH. Expected: 16 (x16). x8 = 50% BW loss. x4 = 75% loss.

panels.title: PCIe: Replay Counter
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_PCIE_REPLAY_COUNTER. Re-transmissions. Rising count = PCIe signal quality degradation.

panels.title: Thermal Violation Duration
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_THERMAL_VIOLATION in microseconds. Any value > 0 = GPU clocks were throttled due to temperature.

panels.title: Power Violation Duration
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_VIOLATION in microseconds. Any value > 0 = GPU clocks throttled due to power cap.

panels.title: InfiniBand: Data Transmitted
panels.description: node_infiniband_port_data_transmitted_bytes_total rate. Should be roughly equal across all HCA ports. Silent port = straggler.

panels.title: InfiniBand: Data Received
panels.description: node_infiniband_port_data_received_bytes_total rate.

panels.title: InfiniBand: Port Errors
panels.description: node_infiniband error counters: symbol, link_downed, rcv_errors.

panels.title: CPU: Utilization
panels.description: 1 - avg(rate(node_cpu_seconds_total{mode='idle'})) — CPU busy percentage.

panels.title: CPU: Load Average
panels.description: node_load1 / node_load5 / node_load15 relative to CPU count.

panels.title: CPU: Temperature
panels.description: node_hwmon_temp_celsius or node_thermal_zone_temp. CPU overheating = MCE risk.

panels.title: Memory: Usage
panels.description: 1 - (MemAvailable / MemTotal). System memory utilization. >95% = OOM risk, container eviction.

panels.title: Memory: ECC Errors (EDAC)
panels.description: node_edac_correctable/uncorrectable_errors_total. Uncorrectable > 0 = DIMM failure, immediate cordon.

panels.title: Memory: Available
panels.description: node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes — actual available memory.

panels.title: Storage: Filesystem Usage
panels.description: node_filesystem_avail_bytes / size. Critical mounts: /, /var, /var/lib/kubelet.

panels.title: Storage: Disk I/O
panels.description: node_disk_io_time_seconds_total rate — disk busy time. Sustained 100% = I/O bottleneck.

panels.title: Storage: Read/Write Throughput
panels.description: node_disk_read/written_bytes_total rate.

panels.title: K8s: Node Conditions
panels.description: kube_node_status_condition — MemoryPressure, DiskPressure, PIDPressure, Ready. Ready=0 or any Pressure=1 = critical.

panels.title: K8s: Node Allocatable vs Capacity
panels.description: kube_node_status_allocatable vs capacity for ephemeral-storage and pods. Large gap = high system reserved.

panels.title: Workload Critical Path — Day 0 Provisioning Gate
Workload Critical Path — Day N Runtime Health
All Checks
panels.description: Every registered check with status, severity, component.

panels.title: Cordon Signal
panels.description: 1 = cordon recommended.

panels.title: Freshness
panels.description: Seconds since last check.
